records.ety: [L. incurrere to run into or toward; pref. in- in + currere to run. See Current.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Incurred (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Incurring (?).]

defs: To meet or fall in with, as something inconvenient, harmful, or onerous; to put one's self in the way of; to expose one's self to; to become liable or subject to; to bring down upon one's self; to encounter; to contract; as, to incur debt, danger, displeasure, penalty, responsibility, etc.
quotes: I know not what I shall incur to pass it, Having no warrant.

defs: To render liable or subject to; to occasion.
quotes: Lest you incur me much more damage in my fame than you have done me pleasure in preserving my life.
Light is discerned by itself because by itself it incurs into the eye.
